title: OpenAI Codex Cheat Sheet
description: A practical guide for engineers using OpenAI Codex for coding assistance.

commands.description: Sets the randomness of the model's output.
commands.scenario: When building a code review bot that needs deterministic output.

commands.description: Selects the model version and enables streaming responses.
commands.scenario: When you need real-time code suggestions in an IDE.

commands.command: Think step by step. Then verify your answer.
commands.description: Encourages the model to reason through problems methodically.
commands.scenario: When debugging complex algorithms and need logical reasoning.

commands.description: Limits the response length to control costs.
commands.scenario: When generating concise code snippets to save on API usage.

commands.description: Balances creativity and determinism in the output.
commands.scenario: When generating creative code solutions with some variability.

commands.command: Use comments to clarify the task.
commands.description: Improves model understanding by providing context.
commands.scenario: When generating complex functions and need precise output.
When generating code with varied function names and structures.

commands.description: Defines a stopping sequence for the model's output.
commands.scenario: When you want to terminate code generation at a specific point.

commands.command: Provide examples of input and output.
commands.description: Guides the model by showing expected patterns.
commands.scenario: When training the model to generate specific code formats.

commands.description: Encourages the model to introduce new concepts.
commands.scenario: When exploring new coding paradigms or libraries.

commands.description: Returns the log probabilities of top tokens.
commands.scenario: When analyzing the model's decision-making process for debugging.

commands.description: Directs the model to generate code with predefined structures.
commands.scenario: When integrating generated code into existing codebases.
When evaluating different code solutions for a problem.

commands.description: Ensures the most deterministic output possible.
commands.scenario: When exact code replication is necessary for compliance or testing.

commands.description: Use nucleus sampling to limit the model's vocabulary to the most likely tokens, improving coherence without the rigidity of temperature=0.
Monitor the 'system_fingerprint' in API responses to detect backend model updates or configuration changes that might affect output consistency.
Instead of just asking to 'think step by step', provide 2-3 completed examples that include the reasoning process within the prompt to guide logic.
Enforce valid JSON output structure by setting the response format, ensuring the model's output is immediately parsable by your application.
Use the 'logit_bias' parameter to increase or decrease the probability of specific tokens appearing, useful for forcing specific syntax or banning certain words.
